title: Globals should not depend on possibly not yet initialized variables
description: This rule raises an issue if the initializer of a global variable a, refers to another global variable b, when there is no guarantee that b is initialized before a. C++ allows global variables to have arbitrally complex initializers. Such variables are zero-initialized statically, and further initialization is performed at runtime, in the process referred to as dynamic initialization.
recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

checks.message: Global variables should not depend on other globals due to undefined initialization order.
